crossRefs.label: Disconnect the headlight leveling sensor from the rear axle beam.
Turn up the interior trunk side lining as necessary.
Headlight initial position learning (vehicles equipped with headlight leveling system)

blocks.text: 3. Headlight leveling sensor disconnection (vehicles equipped with headlight leveling system)
Disconnect the headlight leveling sensor from the rear axle beam.

refs.label: Disconnect the headlight leveling sensor from the rear axle beam.

blocks.text: Apply a jack under the rear axle beam (A) via a waste cloth to support the suspension. Disconnect the damper (B) from the rear axle beam.

items: Do not apply a jack to the center of the rear axle beam (torsion beam).
Spring Lower Mounting The jack should be applied so as not to damage the protrusions on the lower part of the rubber.

items.text: Do not apply a jack to the center of the rear axle beam (torsion beam).
Do not apply a jack to the center of the rear axle beam (torsion beam).
Spring Lower Mounting The jack should be applied so as not to damage the protrusions on the lower part of the rubber.
Spring Lower Mounting The jack should be applied so as not to damage the protrusions on the lower part of the rubber.

blocks.text: Turn up the interior trunk side lining as necessary.

refs.label: Turn up the interior trunk side lining as necessary.

blocks.text: Remove the self-locking nut (A), damper mounting washer (B) and damper mounting rubber (C) from the top of the damper. Loosen the self-locking nut securing the damper rod (E) with an Allen wrench (D).
Remove the damper (A). If necessary, remove the damper mounting rubber (B) from the damper.

Install the damper mounting rubber (A) on the damper (B) if necessary. Insert the upper part of the damper into the vehicle body and place the lower part of the damper on the rear axle beam at the damper mounting position.
Apply a jack under the rear axle beam (A) via a waste cloth to support the suspension. Replace the mounting bolts with new ones, connect the damper (B) to the rear axle beam and tighten temporarily.

items: Do not apply a jack to the center of the rear axle beam (torsion beam).
Spring Lower Mounting The jack should be applied so as not to damage the protrusions on the lower part of the rubber.

items.text: Do not apply a jack to the center of the rear axle beam (torsion beam).
Do not apply a jack to the center of the rear axle beam (torsion beam).
Spring Lower Mounting The jack should be applied so as not to damage the protrusions on the lower part of the rubber.
Spring Lower Mounting The jack should be applied so as not to damage the protrusions on the lower part of the rubber.

blocks.text: Lift the suspension with a jack to apply the vehicle weight and tighten the damper mounting bolts (A) to the specified torque.

Lift the suspension with a jack and apply the vehicle weight, then install the damper mounting rubber (B), damper mounting washer (C) and new self-lock nut (D) on the damper rod (A) from the interior side. Be careful about the mounting direction of washers and rubbers.
Secure the damper rod (B) with the hex wrench (A) and tighten the self-lock nut (C) to the specified torque.
Install the interior trunk side lining.

refs.label: Install the interior trunk side lining.

blocks.text: 7. Headlight leveling sensor installation (for vehicles equipped with headlight leveling system)
Connect the headlight leveling sensor to the rear axle beam.

refs.label: Connect the headlight leveling sensor to the rear axle beam.
